fact_nuggets.text: Leaf Guard (Japanese: Leaf Guard) is an Ability introduced in Generation IV.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Protects the Pokemon from status problems in sunny weather. B S L MD GtI Prevents bad status conditions when it's sunny. * The Pokemon is protected from bad status conditions in the Sunny weather condition! * S MD The Pokemon is protected from bad status conditions when it's sunny! MD RT DX The Pokemon is protected from bad status conditions, such as poison or paralysis, during Sunny weather or in Extremely Harsh Sunlight.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation IV 1.1.2 Generation V onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] During harsh sunlight , Leaf Guard prevents the Pokemon from being afflicted by non-volatile status conditions (including via Flame Orb and Toxic Orb ) and Yawn . It has no effect on any status conditions the Pokemon was afflicted by prior to the weather being set up. If harsh sunlight is created in the turn where a Pokemon made drowsy due to Yawn would fall asleep, the Ability will activate and keep the Pokemon awake. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Rest can still be used normally, and the user falls asleep for two turns. Generation V onward [ edit | edit source ] Rest will now fail if used by a Pokemon with Leaf Guard during harsh sunlight. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Leaf Guard has no effect outside of battle.

Protects against major status ailments during strong sunlight.
